challenges.title: The Golden Lady on Top
challenges.description: At the very top of the Duomo's tallest spire stands the golden symbol of Milan. Go outside, look up, and capture her.
challenges.photoPrompt: Take a photo of La Madonnina, the golden statue on top of the Duomo's tallest spire. Point your camera upward at the central spire.

feedback.correct: Well done. La Madonnina stands above Piazza Duomo and remains one of Milan's most beloved symbols.
feedback.incorrect: The statue is La Madonnina, the golden figure at the highest central spire. Try again from the piazza and look up.

challenges.arHint: Point your camera at the top of the Duomo's central facade and look for the highest spire.

challenges.title: The Floor That Brings Luck
challenges.description: Hidden in the Galleria's mosaic floor is a tradition that visitors travel from all over the world to perform.
challenges.photoPrompt: Find the mosaic bull emblem on the Galleria floor and take a photo of it. Look near the centre of the octagonal crossing.

feedback.correct: Well spotted. The bull is the emblem of Turin, and the worn mosaic shows how many visitors have spun there for luck.
feedback.incorrect: The mosaic bull represents Turin. Head to the central octagon of the Galleria and look down.

challenges.arHint: Look for the centre of the octagonal crossing, where the four city crests meet.
challenges.location: Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II - central octagon

challenges.title: The Ballroom That Was Never Rebuilt
challenges.description: Inside Palazzo Reale there is a room bombed in 1943 and deliberately left in ruins as a war memorial.
challenges.photoPrompt: Find the Sala delle Cariatidi, the bombed ballroom left unrestored after 1943, and take a photo of the room.

feedback.correct: Exactly. The Sala delle Cariatidi was left scarred as a memorial to wartime destruction.
feedback.incorrect: The room is called the Sala delle Cariatidi. Ask staff for directions or look for the unrestored ballroom space.

challenges.arHint: Stand facing Palazzo Reale and look for clues about its wartime damage and restoration.

challenges.title: When the First Stone Was Laid
challenges.description: Standing in front of the Duomo, you are looking at six centuries of construction. Tell us when it began.
challenges.question: In what year did construction of the Duomo di Milano begin?

feedback.correct: Correct: 1386. Construction continued for nearly six centuries.
feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The first stone was laid in 1386.

challenges.arHint: Stand facing the cathedral's main facade and think about its medieval origins.

challenges.title: The Architect of the Arcade
challenges.description: The Galleria's iron-and-glass roof was a marvel of its century. Who designed it?
challenges.question: Who designed the Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II?

feedback.correct: Right: Giuseppe Mengoni designed the Galleria between 1865 and 1877.

challenges.arHint: Look up at the iron-and-glass dome and the way the four arms meet at the octagon.
challenges.location: Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II - central octagon

challenges.title: Saffron Sensation
challenges.description: A Milanese classic gets its golden colour from one unmistakable seasoning.
challenges.question: Which yellow seasoning is used in traditional risotto alla Milanese?

feedback.correct: Correct. Saffron gives risotto alla Milanese its golden colour.

challenges.title: Palazzo Reale Memorial
challenges.description: One damaged room in Palazzo Reale still tells the story of wartime Milan.
challenges.question: Palazzo Reale left a ballroom unrepaired as a memorial after what major event?

feedback.correct: Correct. The wartime bombing left scars that became part of the palace's memory.
feedback.incorrect: The answer is World War II, specifically the wartime bombing damage.

challenges.title: Galleria Vision
challenges.description: The Galleria floor carries emblems of Italian cities. One famous mosaic shows a bull.
challenges.question: Which city is represented by the bull mosaic on the Galleria floor?
challenges.location: Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II - central octagon
